steps.title: Identify the problem in the notes
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a potential problem. In the final note, Karst and colleagues articulate their concern: recent claims about CMNs are “disconnected from evidence.” This is the critical negative assessment about the theory.

steps.title: Apply the concern to the choices
steps.content: Choice C uses the quotation “disconnected from evidence” to show the gap between claims and scientific evidence. The other choices either quote positive language — Simard's “super-cooperators” (A) or Karst's “captured the interest” (B) — or provide no quotation at all (D). That matches choice C — C is correct.
